name: Rice (food medium)
basis: per 100 g raw milled white long-grain rice grain (Oryza sativa), dry weight

components.name: starch
components.source: USDA FDC 169756 (Rice, white, long-grain, regular, raw, unenriched); computed as total carbohydrate 80 g minus free sugars 0.12 g minus fibre ~0.5 g
components.evidence: THE dominant carbon/energy source of rice by an overwhelming margin: an amylose+amylopectin glucan reservoir that microbes access only after amylolytic hydrolysis to maltose/glucose. Amylose fraction is ~15-30% of starch and is cultivar-dependent (low in glutinous, high in long-grain indica).

components.name: total free sugars
components.evidence: Free (non-starch) sugars are trivial in milled rice (~0.1-0.35% w/w; Perez & Juliano, Phytochemistry 1978 for milled rice soluble sugar 0.21-0.35%). Predominantly sucrose, with glucose, fructose and maltose in trace. Starch, not free sugar, is the fermentable carbon.

components.name: sucrose
components.source: Perez & Juliano 1978 (milled rice, sucrose is the principal free sugar); USDA FDC 169756 total sugars 0.12 g
components.evidence: Predominant free sugar of milled rice but individual value not resolved by USDA; total free sugars only 0.12 g/100g so contribution is minor. Included qualitatively.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Trace free monosaccharide; the major glucose pool is bound in starch (see starch).

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Trace free monosaccharide in milled rice.

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: Trace in raw grain; becomes the major amylolysis product once starch is enzymatically degraded.

components.name: phytic acid (myo-inositol hexakisphosphate)
components.source: Nkhata et al. (Malawian white rice) 93-205 mg/100g; Liang et al. review PMC3959956 (endosperm phytate-P ~0.63 mg/g DW)
components.evidence: The dominant organic acid / phosphate storage compound remaining in milled rice; midpoint of the reported white-rice range (93-205 mg/100g, variety-dependent). Chelates Fe/Zn/Ca (lowering their bioavailability) and is a bound-phosphate reservoir. MISSING from the FooDB profile.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Heinemann et al., processing-of-rice composition literature (organic acids decline with milling)
components.evidence: Present as a minor free organic acid, decreasing progressively with milling; not cleanly quantified for milled white rice. Included qualitatively.

components.name: malic acid
components.evidence: Minor free organic acid of the grain, reduced by milling; not quantified for milled white rice. Included qualitatively.

components.name: L-glutamate (glutamic acid)
components.evidence: Most abundant protein-derived amino acid in rice (rice protein is glutelin-rich); major N/C source once protein is hydrolysed.

components.name: L-lysine
components.evidence: Rice is a lysine-limited protein (first-limiting essential amino acid), relevant for auxotroph growth.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Predominantly glutelin (oryzenin) plus prolamin; the amino acids above are the protein-hydrolysate profile.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Very low-fat; residual endosperm and surface lipid after milling.

components.name: oleic acid (18:1 n-9)
components.evidence: One of the two dominant fatty acids of milled rice lipid.

components.name: palmitic acid (16:0)
components.evidence: Dominant saturated fatty acid of rice.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Major polyunsaturated fatty acid; co-dominant with oleic.

components.name: phosphorus (phosphate)
components.evidence: A large share is bound as phytate-P (see phytic acid), so free bioavailable phosphate is lower than total.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Single consolidated iron value; the FooDB Fe2+/Fe3+/'Fe2+ mitochondria' entries are compartment/oxidation-state duplicates of this one nutrient.

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: Notably selenium-rich for a cereal; value is soil-dependent.

components.name: niacin (vitamin B3)
components.evidence: Values are for UNENRICHED rice; enriched/fortified rice carries much higher B1/B3/folate/Fe.

components.name: thiamin (vitamin B1)
components.evidence: Low in milled rice (thiamin is concentrated in the removed bran/germ).

components.name: vitamin E (alpha-tocopherol)
components.evidence: Low in milled endosperm; note white-rice vitamin E is actually tocotrienol-predominant (gamma-tocotrienol > alpha-tocopherol; Liang et al. PMC3959956).

components.name: vitamin K (phylloquinone)
components.evidence: Trace.

components.name: glycine betaine
components.source: FooDB FOOD00125; rice is a known glycine-betaine accumulator
components.evidence: Genuine rice osmolyte/methyl donor; present but not quantified in FDC. Included qualitatively.

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: Toxic metalloid contaminant (rice accumulates inorganic arsenic from paddy soil/water); a food-safety concern, not a growth nutrient.

remove.name: Cholesterol
remove.reason: Animal sterol, biologically implausible in a plant/cereal food; FooDB predicted-not-measured entry.

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: FooDB compartment/oxidation-state duplicate of iron; consolidated into the single 'iron' component (0.8 mg/100g).

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: Duplicate oxidation-state entry of the same iron nutrient; consolidated into 'iron'.

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Cobalt: ultratrace generic-element padding, not quantified/nutritionally meaningful in milled rice.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Ultratrace generic-element padding, not a meaningful rice growth nutrient.

remove.name: Iodine c
remove.reason: Ultratrace; not a meaningful/quantified nutrient of rice.

remove.name: Fluoride
remove.reason: Ultratrace/soil-water dependent, not a meaningful rice growth nutrient.

remove.name: Molybdate
remove.reason: Ultratrace generic-element padding, not quantified for milled rice.

missing_added: starch (78 g/100g) made explicit as THE dominant fermentable carbon source
phytic acid (~150 mg/100g) — the dominant organic acid/phosphate store, absent from FooDB profile
quantitative fatty-acid profile (palmitic, oleic, linoleic, alpha-linolenic, stearic)
quantitative mineral panel (P, K, Mg, Ca, Na, Fe, Zn, Mn, Cu, Se)
quantitative vitamin panel (B1, B2, B3, B5, B6, folate, E, K, choline)
citric and malic acid flagged as present-but-minor (decline with milling)
keep_uncovered: genistein / isoflavones (genuine rice phytochemicals and phytoalexins; no BiGG metabolite)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is STARCH (~78 g/100g, amylose+amylopectin) accessed via amylolysis to maltose/glucose; free sugars are trivial (0.12 g/100g, mostly sucrose). Rice is NOT an organic-acid-rich food: the one notable organic acid is phytic acid (~150 mg/100g), with citric/malic only trace and largely lost to milling. Values are for raw, unenriched, milled white long-grain rice (USDA FDC 169756); enriched rice would carry substantially higher B1/B3/folate/iron. Confidence high for USDA macro/AA/mineral/vitamin panel; medium for phytic acid (variety spread 93-205 mg/100g) and for the qualitative sugar/organic-acid entries.
